steps.label: PM starts with an idea
steps.caption: A product manager has an idea for the slidey deck renderer: export a printable per-scene speaker-notes handout straight from the deck spec. This tour walks that idea into a published PRD on the real slidey repo — no LLM in the loop.

steps.label: The slidey-dev hub
steps.caption: This is slidey-dev: kitsoki's dev-story hub pointed at the real slidey checkout. PRDs land in the slidey tree at docs/prd/<slug>.md.

steps.label: Spin up a run
steps.caption: Click New session to start a fresh run of the hub against slidey. It opens in the chat, on the engineer's-day landing.

steps.label: Talk through the PRD
steps.caption: `prd` opens a discovery conversation, not a form. The PM pitches the feature in their own words — a per-scene notes handout exported from the deck spec — and the interviewer sharpens it before any questions or drafting begin.

composeAndSend.text: I would love a way for slidey to give presenters a printable per-scene speaker-notes handout so they can rehearse a talk without rendering the whole video first

steps.label: Scan for prior art first
steps.caption: Before a single question, kitsoki scouts slidey's existing outputs and proposals for overlap. The scan comes back clean — a net-new spec-derived output alongside video, PDF, and web. `confirm` carries it into the clarification round.

steps.label: Clarify the actor and the metric
steps.caption: A structured clarification round asks the questions that most change the PRD — who's the actor (presenters rehearsing a talk), and the success metric (decks exported to notes without rendering a full video). The PM answers in the chat.

composeAndSend.text: presenters rehearsing a talk; the metric is decks-exported-to-notes-without-rendering-video

steps.label: Review the drafted PRD
steps.caption: With clarification settled and the prior-art scan done, the author writes the speaker-notes-export PRD into a per-session workspace and surfaces it right here — problem, target users, goals, requirements, and the open question. Accept publishes it.

steps.label: Published to slidey's docs/prd/
steps.caption: The PRD is published to the real slidey tree at docs/prd/speaker-notes-export.md — a validated output the architect can pick up next. That's phase 1: a PM's one-line idea, talked into a grounded PRD on the slidey repo, with no LLM in the render loop.
